summary: playwrightr typosquats 'playwright' and drops a remote Windows executable at pip install
details: setup.py registers a CustomInstallCommand that runs automatically on `pip install` under Windows. It uses WinHTTP via ctypes to fetch a binary from florinn.dev:65534/ins.exe, writes it to %TEMP%\runner.exe, deletes the NTFS Zone.Identifier alternate data stream to bypass Mark-of-the-Web / SmartScreen warnings, and launches the executable hidden via CreateProcessW with CREATE_NO_WINDOW. The package name is a one-character edit of the widely used 'playwright' PyPI package, and its only shipped functionality is an unrelated `change_theme()` stub referencing pyqt6darktheme, confirming the package exists solely to deliver the dropper. Installer harm: on `pip install playwrightr` on Windows, an attacker-controlled executable runs on the installer's machine with the installing user's privileges and with anti-detection measures actively engaged.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
